perturbation.original_text: 1.1 The term "AETRU System" means an integrated all-electric vehicle refrigeration system comprising of Product as the refrigeration mechanism and the AuraGen as the power-supply.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 The term "AETRU System" means an integrated vehicle system.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting "all-electric vehicle refrigeration system comprising of Product as the refrigeration mechanism and the AuraGen as the power-supply" from the AETRU System definition, the term's scope becomes ambiguous. The text, as it is, doesn't specify what that system does and that it is all-electric. This is contradictory because several clauses elsewhere rely on the explicit understanding that AETRU Systems are specifically all-electric vehicle refrigeration systems using the AuraGen as the power supply. Making that explicit definition creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Appointment. Pursuant to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement, Aura appoints Zanotti as its exclusive supplier of the Products within the Territory and Field of Use and Zanotti hereby accepts such appointment. In order to maintain the exclusivity granted hereunder, Zanotti shall provide Aura with orders for a minimum of (i) one thousand (1,000) AETRU Systems during the first twenty-four (24) months of this Agreement and (ii) seven hundred and fifty (750) AETRU Systems per year thereafter for so long as this Agreement remains in effect (the "Minimum Order"). In the event that Zanotti fails to secure purchases amounting to the Minimum Order for any particular period, the exclusive supplier rights granted pursuant to this Article 2 shall become non- exclusive commencing immediately following such period in which the Minimum Order was not achieved and Aura shall have full discretion to purchase or otherwise obtain Product from sources other than Zanotti. Aura shall serve as seller for all orders for AETRU Systems (whether such orders are provided by Zanotti or otherwise) and shall directly invoice all buyers and collect the purchase price for all AETRU Systems sold.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the crucial detail that the "AETRU System" is an "all-electric vehicle refrigeration system comprising of Product as the refrigeration mechanism and the AuraGen as the power-supply". The contradicted text relies on this specific definition when stipulating that Zanotti must provide orders for a minimum number of "AETRU Systems". Without the original definition, the Minimum Order becomes unenforceable as it's unclear what constitutes an 'AETRU System,' thus contradicting the exclusivity clause.

perturbation.original_text: 3.7 End-User Warranty. At all times during the Term of this Agreement, Zanotti shall warrant the Products to purchasers of AETRU Systems in accordance with the terms of its standard warranty attached hereto as Exhibit "C" ("End User Warranty"), as such End User Warranty may be modified from time to time by Zanotti, provided however that at no time shall such End User Warranty provide for a warranty coverage period of less than thirty-six (36) months from the time of purchase by the initial end-user. A copy of the End User Warranty shall be included as part of the packaging included with all Product delivered to Aura and Aura shall distribute each Product with all warranty cards, a copy of the End User Warranty and all other (a) Deliveries may be made in partial shipments. With respect to all deliveries (partial or otherwise) Zanotti shall invoice each Product separately. (b) Title and risk of loss shall pass to Aura at the time the Products are tendered by Zanotti to the initial carrier and Aura alone shall be responsible for all freight, customs duties, insurance or other shipping costs. (c) In the absence of specific instructions from Aura, Zanotti shall select a reasonable carrier but shall not thereby assume any liability in connection with shipment, nor shall the carrier be construed to be the agent of Zanotti. packaging materials intact. Additionally, Zanotti shall offer to end-users the option to purchase extended warranty coverage for an additional twenty-four (24) months - making the total warranty period sixty (60) months.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 End-User Warranty. Zanotti will include a warranty of its choosing with each product. Additionally, Zanotti shall offer to end-users the option to purchase extended warranty coverage.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the explicit reference to "Exhibit C" and the specific minimum warranty period (36 months), the clause creates uncertainty. The text still refers to the existence of a warranty but removes crucial details like the terms of its standard warranty or how it may be modified, which is stated in the original clause. This removal creates in-text contradiction, because it is now unclear what the end-user warranty is as it is not clearly defined in the exhibit nor there is a minimum warranty period, making this uneforcable. It creates an unclarity with what type of waranty and coverage the purchaser of the AETRU system is going to be covered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.7 End-User Warranty. Zanotti will include a warranty of its choosing with each product. Additionally, Zanotti shall offer to end-users the option to purchase extended warranty coverage.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the explicit reference to 'Exhibit C' and the specific minimum warranty period (36 months). This omission creates a contradiction with the original intent and expectations set within the initial agreement. While the changed text mentions a warranty, it lacks the crucial details present in the original version, like the warranty's terms or how it may be modified. The contradictory section is the changed text itself, as it fails to provide the details that would ensure a clear and enforceable warranty, making it unclear what the end-user warranty is.

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 The term "Products" means collectively those transport refrigeration systems listed in Exhibit A attached hereto, as well as any future modifications, enhancements or improvements thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 The term "Products" means any items sold by Zanotti.
perturbation.explanation: By broadening the definition of "Products" to mean any items sold by Zanotti and removing specific transport refrigeration systems listed in Exhibit A, this creates an internal contradiction. This is because other sections in the contract mention Products as integrated all-electric transport refrigeration solution and not 'any items sold by Zanotti.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Appointment. Pursuant to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement, Aura appoints Zanotti as its exclusive supplier of the Products within the Territory and Field of Use and Zanotti hereby accepts such appointment. In order to maintain the exclusivity granted hereunder, Zanotti shall provide Aura with orders for a minimum of (i) one thousand (1,000) AETRU Systems during the first twenty-four (24) months of this Agreement and (ii) seven hundred and fifty (750) AETRU Systems per year thereafter for so long as this Agreement remains in effect (the "Minimum Order"). In the event that Zanotti fails to secure purchases amounting to the Minimum Order for any particular period, the exclusive supplier rights granted pursuant to this Article 2 shall become non- exclusive commencing immediately following such period in which the Minimum Order was not achieved and Aura shall have full discretion to purchase or otherwise obtain Product from sources other than Zanotti. Aura shall serve as seller for all orders for AETRU Systems (whether such orders are provided by Zanotti or otherwise) and shall directly invoice all buyers and collect the purchase price for all AETRU Systems sold.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Products' as specific transport refrigeration systems listed in Exhibit A. The changed text broadens this definition to 'any items sold by Zanotti.' This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which refers to 'Products' in the context of 'AETRU Systems', implying a more specific definition than just 'any items sold by Zanotti'. The minimum order quantities are specifically for AETRU Systems, not for any other item Zanotti might sell, creating a contradiction regarding the scope of the agreement and the minimum order obligations. If Zanotti sells other items, are those included in the Minimum Order? No. The change introduces a clear conflict, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.
